title: elaeagnus multiflora var. tsukubana
intro: Elaeagnus multiflora var. tsukubana, also known as the Tsukuba silverberry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, characterized by its silvery-gray leaves and clusters of small, fragrant flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and skin irritations. Consuming the fruit of the Tsukuba silverberry has been linked to various health benefits, including improved immune function, reduced stress levels, and enhanced antioxidant defenses. In herbal preparations, Elaeagnus multiflora var. tsukubana is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to support overall well-being and promote a healthy balance of gut flora.
